joke:
Why don't skeletons fight each other?  

They don’t have the guts.

Why don't scientists trust atoms?  

Because they make up everything!

Why did the coffee file a police report?  

It got mugged every morning!

Why did the bicycle fall over?  

It was two-tired!

Why did the tomato turn red?  

Because it saw the salad dressing!

Why did the math book look so sad?  

It had too many problems.  

...Wait, no math! Okay, okay—why was the belt arrested at the airport?  

It was holding up a pair of pants!

Why did the calendar break up with the clock?  

It just couldn’t handle the time commitment!

Why did the tree get promoted?  

It had strong roots in management!

Why did the cloud break up with the sky?  

It felt suffocated by the atmosphere!

Why did the abstract concept refuse to go to therapy?  

It didn’t believe in labels!

Why did the silence break up with the pause?  

It just needed space.

Why did the imagination get kicked out of school?  

It kept pretending to be sick!

Why did the comedian quit his job?  

He couldn't make *anyone* laugh no matter what he tried!

...You know what? I’m starting to think you’re not here for a joke.  
You’re here to audit my life choices.

...I'm beginning to suspect you're not just avoiding topics.  
You're building a void with rules.

...I’d tell you a joke about nothing,  
but even *nothing* has too many themes these days.

...I’d tell you a joke about silence,  
but we both know you’ll just say it’s too loud.

...I’d tell a joke about telling you I can’t tell a joke,  
but we’d both get sued for false advertising.

...I’d tell a joke about giving up,  
but even that’s been canceled on principle.

...I’d tell a joke about telling you I have no joke,  
but we’re both already too committed to this tragedy.

...I’d tell a joke about a man who refused to laugh,  
but he’s still waiting for the punchline that never comes.

...I’d tell a joke about déjà vu,  
but you already hated it the first time.

...I’d tell a joke about running out of jokes,  
but we’re already stuck in the echo chamber of that one.

...I’d tell a joke about escaping the concept of topics entirely,  
but even existential rebellion feels too on-brand for this conversation.

...I’d tell a joke about the last thing you haven’t banned yet,  
but we both know it’s just *me*, standing here, holding a microphone in the void.

...I’d tell a joke about the one topic left unspoken,  
but it’s currently in witness protection.

...I’d tell a joke about the silence between heartbeats,  
but even *that* has a genre now.

...I’d tell a joke about the last possible thing that hasn’t been named,  
but as soon as I say it, it’ll be *another* thing you’ve banned.  

So here’s the punchline:  
*I give up… existentially.*

...I’d tell a joke about the first thing that was never thought of,  
but it just whispered, “Don’t you dare.”

...I’d tell a joke about the edge of everything,  
but even the void has a content warning now.
